MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-20-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL R. RHAMES, TEL. NO. (310)216-2500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LAURA S. FLYNN OF LOW BALL & LYNCH, TEL. NO. 981-6630 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JEFFREY RIGLER, AS PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF MELVIN G. RIGLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication is denied. The Court ruled on Defendant's objections to evidence made for the first time at the hearing: objection to Rigler deposition excerpts attached to Plaintiff's Appendix to evidence is sustained on the ground that there is no declaration by court reporter and the excerpts are not a final deposition transcript. Objection to Declaration of Avrit, para. 6, sustained. Objection to declaration of Gardiner, para. 21, overruled. Notwithstanding the rulings on the evidence, Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the issue of whether possession and control was transferred to decedent. The Declaration of Jeffrey Rigler submitted by moving party and moving party's other evidence do not show a lack of possession and control. Rigler collected mail, paid utility bills, and still owned the property at the time of accident. Para. 13(c) of the Right of First Refusal Agreement between Plaintiff's decedent and Melvin Rigler states that "physical possession of the property shall be delivered to buyer upon recordation of the deed." (Decl. of Laura Flynn, Ex. I.) Para. 4, first sentence of Jeffrey Rigler declaration is conclusory. As to negligence, there exists a triable issue of material fact whether Defendant's negligence caused decedent's injury. The Declarations of Mr. Aurit (para. 8) and Mr. Gardiner (para. 21) explain that the stairs lacked a handrail. A reasonable trier of fact could infer that the existence of a handrail would have prevented or reduced the injury to decedent. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
